fingerprints.method_out_of_state: IdentoGO Out-of-State Live Scan (adds ~$39.95 fee) OR FD-258 hard card via mail (tip: moisturize hands for days prior to reduce smudge rejects)

seo.title: West Virginia Respiratory Therapist License Reciprocity - How to Transfer Your License (2026)
seo.description: Already licensed as a respiratory therapist? Here's whether your license transfers to West Virginia - compact status, endorsement steps, fees, and processing time.
seo.keywords: respiratory therapist license reciprocity west virginia, how to transfer respiratory therapist license to west virginia, respiratory therapist compact west virginia, west virginia respiratory therapist endorsement application

board.name: West Virginia Board of Respiratory Care
board.mailing_address:
West Virginia Board of Respiratory Care Practitioners
101 Dee Drive, Suite 120
Charleston, WV 25311

quick_facts.exam_name: National Board for Respiratory Care (NBRC) examination

license_types.title: Registered Respiratory Therapist (RRT)
license_types.description: The standard credential to practice as a respiratory therapist in West Virginia.

steps.title: Register and Initiate Application
steps.description: Visit the WVBRC Online Licensing System at https://apps.wv.gov/wvbrc/licenseapplication/licensetype. Create a user account if you are a new applicant, then log in. Select 'New Individual License Application' and choose 'Respiratory Care Practitioner' to begin your application.

steps.title: Complete Online Application and Pay Fee
steps.description: Fill out all sections of the application accurately within the WVBRC Online Licensing System. This includes personal, educational, and professional history. Pay the required application fee online via credit/debit card.

steps.title: Submit Supporting Documents
steps.description: Arrange for official transcripts from your COARC-accredited respiratory care program to be sent directly to the Board. Also, ensure your NBRC RRT credential verification is accessible or submitted as required.

steps.title: Complete Background Check
steps.description: Follow the instructions provided by the Board to complete a state and federal background check, which typically involves fingerprinting through an approved vendor.

steps.title: Monitor and Respond
steps.description: Regularly log into your WVBRC Online Licensing System account to monitor the status of your application and respond promptly to any requests for additional information or clarification from the Board.

documents.detail: Sent directly from your educational institution.
Official verification of passing the NBRC (National Board for Respiratory Care) TMC Exam.

provisional_text: Many states offer a limited permit or provisional license for new graduates waiting to take the NBRC. Check specific board rules for supervisory requirements.
reciprocity_text: Respiratory Therapists licensed in another state can often apply via endorsement by providing license verification and current certification.

faqs.question: How much does a West Virginia respiratory therapist license cost?
faqs.answer: The initial application and licensing fee varies, often around $100 to $200. Check the specific fee breakdown above.

faqs.question: Is national certification required?
faqs.answer: Yes, passing the NBRC (National Board for Respiratory Care) TMC Exam is a standard requirement for initial licensure across almost all states.

faqs.question: Does West Virginia have reciprocity for respiratory therapists?
faqs.answer: No interstate compact currently covers respiratory therapist practice in West Virginia. To work in West Virginia, apply for licensure by endorsement through the state board.

faqs.question: How long does it take to transfer my respiratory therapist license to West Virginia?
faqs.answer: Most endorsement applications take 4 to 6 Weeks.

faqs.question: Can I start working in West Virginia while my endorsement application is processing?
faqs.answer: A temporary or provisional credential is available while the full endorsement application is pending.

reciprocity.notes: Respiratory Therapists licensed in another state can often apply via endorsement by providing license verification and current certification.

hero.title: Become a Respiratory Therapist in West Virginia
hero.dek: To apply for a Respiratory Care Practitioner license in West Virginia, you must use the West Virginia Board of Respiratory Care Practitioners Online Licensing System.

official_links.portal_name: West Virginia Board of Respiratory Care Practitioners Online Licensing System
official_links.registration_steps: Visit the WVBRC Online Licensing System. Click on 'New Individual License Application', then select 'Respiratory Care Practitioner'. If you are a new user, you will be prompted to create an account before proceeding with the application.
